headline: Alphabet joins Dow Jones Industrial Average, shares rise 3.7%
summary: Investing.com -- Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL) shares rose 3.7% to $350.24 on Monday as the Google parent company began trading as a member of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ).

headline: Alphabet debuts in Dow Jones Industrial Average as index tilts toward tech
summary: Alphabet shares climbed on Monday as the Google parent made its debut in the blue-chip Dow Jones ‌Industrial Average, replacing Verizon Communications, and immediately ranking among its ‌most influential members.  • Its shares rose 3.7% to $350.24, offering one of the biggest boosts ​to the 30-member Dow.  • The company replaced Verizon on the index, S&P Dow Jones Indices said in an announcement on June 23.

headline: Sherwin-Williams Selects Square as Newest Partner in Digital Alliance Program
summary: DISTRIBUTED-WORKFORCE/OAKLAND, Calif., June 29, 2026--Square today announced that The Sherwin-Williams Company, the world’s largest paint and coatings company, has selected Square as its payment solutions partner in the Sherwin-Williams Digital Alliance Program. The curated program connects Sherwin-Williams’ extensive network of PRO+ customers with best-in-class digital tools designed to help grow their businesses and save money through exclusive offers.

headline: Does Sherwin-Williams’ (SHW) Index Exit Reveal a Shift in Its Risk-Reward Profile?
summary: In late June 2026, The Sherwin-Williams Company was removed from the Russell 1000 Dynamic Index, altering its role in that benchmark’s composition. This index removal can matter for investors because funds tracking the Russell 1000 Dynamic Index may reassess or adjust their Sherwin-Williams holdings. We’ll now examine how Sherwin-Williams’ removal from the Russell 1000 Dynamic Index could influence its existing investment narrative and risk profile. We've uncovered the 8 dividend fortresses...
